meta_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS starter kit to Solve no project ideas
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no project ideas' trap. Use this SaaS starter kit guide to build, launch, and monetize your MVP using Antigravity and Claude Code today.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for the 'perfect' idea. Use a high-quality SaaS starter kit to build a functional MVP in hours, not weeks. By leveraging Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for rapid iteration, you shift your focus from coding boilerplate to validating market demand and securing your first paying customer.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank IDE, the cursor blinking in a rhythmic taunt. You have the technical skills to build, but the 'no project ideas' paralysis is the silent killer of every aspiring founder. It isn't a lack of creativity; it is a lack of a framework. While you wait for a lightning bolt of inspiration, your competitors are shipping, iterating, and collecting recurring revenue.

Real founders don't wait for inspiration; they build infrastructure. By adopting a battle-tested SaaS starter kit, you bypass the soul-crushing setup phase and get straight to the only thing that matters: solving a specific problem for a specific user. This is how you transform from a tinkerer into a software entrepreneur.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Architecture
steps.description: Initialize your project with a pre-configured stack that includes auth, billing, and database connectivity. This eliminates 40 hours of boilerplate.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new SaaS boilerplate with Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe integration. Include a clean landing page and a protected dashboard route.

steps.title: Define the Micro-SaaS Problem
steps.description: Use AI to analyze high-frequency pain points in niche subreddits or developer communities to find your first viable MVP feature.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the following problem space and suggest 3 high-demand, low-complexity features for a B2B productivity tool.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "analyze market trends for productivity tools and generate a feature list"

steps.title: Rapid Feature Implementation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to generate the core business logic while you focus on the UI/UX flow of your application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a database schema and API route for the selected feature, ensuring it connects to the existing Supabase auth middleware.

steps.title: Monetization Integration
steps.description: Implement Stripe checkout sessions immediately. Don't build a product without a way to capture value.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure Stripe Webhooks to update user subscription status in the database upon successful checkout.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "configure stripe webhook handlers and update user table on success"

steps.title: Deployment and Feedback Loop
steps.description: Ship the MVP to a staging environment and get it in front of 5 potential users to validate your hypothesis.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment configuration for Vercel and a feedback collection form for the dashboard.

pro_tips: Prioritize 'boring' problems; enterprise users pay for solutions to administrative headaches, not novel AI experiments.
Ship the billing system before the product; if nobody clicks the 'buy' button, you've saved yourself weeks of development.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your authentication flow before you write a single line of business logic.
Avoid 'feature creep' by enforcing a strict 48-hour limit to reach a functional MVP state.
Always include a 'Request a Feature' button in your MVP; it is the cheapest way to get a product roadmap.
content.next_steps: After completing your MVP, push to Vercel, share your link on Twitter/X within the #buildinpublic community, and reach out to 10 cold leads to gather qualitative feedback.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for the sake of building. Start building for revenue. Your first successful exit starts with a single, validated commit. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
